body.title: Announce broker: validate proof field before issuing token (prevent identity spoofing)
body.result: Already implemented: AnnounceBroker.resolve_announce() validates proof field for _protected_agents() at broker.py:128-134; test_announce_proof_validation.py confirms; proof='test-proof-stub' added to igor_envelope fixture
body.description:
HIGH gap found in T-murderbot-security-review.

AnnounceBroker.resolve_announce() issues a token to any IdentityEnvelope that names a recognized agent_id. The proof field is stored in the envelope but never validated. Any process -- including a ContainerShim-tier untrusted agent -- can announce as agent_id='igor' or agent_id='cc' and receive a token granting them that identity. This allows: (a) trust tier gaming (tier_2 memory writes attributed to Igor), (b) receiving Igor's tool bindings and ACL, (c) gaming allowed_actions in the policy gate.

The proof field (IdentityEnvelope.proof: dict) is the design's own intended identity hook (see comment: 'locality trust for v1'). It is currently a no-op.

v1 fix: at minimum, reject announces where agent_id is in a 'protected' set (igor, cc, skeleton) and proof is empty. The protected agent types have known launch paths (tmux session, cc process, skeleton boot) and can supply a shared secret or challenge-response. For non-protected agent_ids, proof remains optional.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/announce/broker.py (resolve_announce), unseen_university/announce/envelope.py (proof field docs), config/profiles/ (protected agent list)
**Design rules:** flat-file registry; no Postgres dependency in announce path
**Scope boundary:** IN -- reject protected-agent announces with empty proof; define proof schema for protected agents; OUT -- full challenge-response PKI, non-protected agent proof enforcement
**Completion criteria:** an announce envelope claiming agent_id='igor' with empty proof is rejected with AnnounceError; an announce with valid proof passes; existing test agents (igor fixture in tests) updated to supply proof
